title: Glorious Golden Venezuelan Plantain Skewers
description: A charred and bouncy Venezuelan dish featuring kinako and cure, perfect for a monsoon evening, this dairy-free-friendly recipe is a must try. The combination of plantains, kinako, and spices creates a unique flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Peel the plantains and cut them into 1-inch pieces.
Thread the plantain pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
In a small bowl, mix together the kinako, cure, coconut oil, lime juice, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the kinako mixture onto the plantain pieces.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the plantains are charred and tender.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional kinako and cure if desired.
Let the skewers cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve with your favorite dipping sauce.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
